personSummary.value: Former home cook who responds warmly to the nickname Emi-san, old enka songs, warm soup, and a calm mealtime pace. Intake improves when food is warm and staff sit nearby without hurrying. Cold soup and late trays can lead to refusal or turning away. Warm water and nickname use support evening medication acceptance.

citations.quote: accepted more when the bowl was warmed

value.description: Recent lunch notes show cold soup reducing intake until the bowl is warmed.

citations.quote: soup cooled; resident refused several bites

value.description: Nickname use and warm water supported medication acceptance.

citations.quote: used her preferred nickname before explaining the dose

value: Use the nickname Emi-san before giving meal or medication instructions.
Check that soup and drinks are warm before encouraging intake.
Play a familiar enka song during afternoon tea or slow transitions.

citations.quote: hummed along to an old enka song

value: Cold soup or delayed meal trays.
Medication explanations that start without her preferred nickname.

citations.quote: staff sat beside her without hurrying

value.situation: Lunch tray is delayed or soup has cooled
value.approach: Replace or warm the soup before prompting additional bites.

citations.quote: replaced the soup with a warmer bowl and intake improved

value.approach: Start with Emi-san, offer warm water, then explain one dose step calmly.

citations.quote: accepted after staff offered warm water

handoffBrief.value: Emiko Watanabe needs warm food, a slow mealtime pace, and the nickname Emi-san; cold soup or late trays can reduce intake, while enka songs and warm water help transitions.

value.claim: Watch lunch intake on days when trays are delayed or soup cools before serving.
